description: Republican office looking for paid interns for the Summer 2020 semester.
responsibilities: Serve as a paid intern for the Summer 2020 semester
qualifications: Availability for the Summer 2020 semester
Interest in congressional work

position_title: Legal Or Graduate Student Summer Internship
office: Select Committee on the Climate Crisis
description: The Majority Staff of the Select Committee on the Climate Crisis seeks candidates for a full or part-time legal or graduate student summer internship, beginning in May or June, running through August 2020. Responsibilities may include conducting legislative research, producing analyses on proposed climate and environmental policies, attending briefings and hearings for staff, and other tasks as assigned or needed. The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, self-motivated, reliable, and have excellent writing and communications skills. An interest in environmental law or policy is strongly desired; this internship is geared towards those entering their 2L or 3L year, or those enrolled in MPP programs, as of Fall 2020.
responsibilities: Produce analyses on proposed climate and environmental policies
Attend briefings and hearings for staff
Perform other tasks as assigned or needed
qualifications: Detail-oriented
Self-motivated
Reliable
Excellent writing and communications skills
Interest in environmental law or policy strongly desired
Entering 2L or 3L year or enrolled in MPP programs as of Fall 2020
how_to_apply: Please send a resume and cover letter to SCCCDjobs@mail.house.gov with "Summer Legal/Grad Student Intern" in the subject line by March 6, 2020. Please include your approximate dates of availability in the message, and how many days per week you would be available.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer, and diverse candidates are encouraged to apply.

office: Select Committee on the Climate Crisis
description: The Majority Staff of the Select Committee on the Climate Crisis seeks full or part-time candidates for summer internships, beginning in May or June, running through August 2020.
responsibilities: Conduct administrative tasks for the committee
Attend briefings and hearings for staff
Prepare and assemble materials for Committee hearings and events
Perform other tasks as assigned or needed
qualifications: Detail-oriented, self-motivated, and reliable
Excellent writing and communications skills
Interest in environmental, climate change, or science policy strongly desired and preferred
how_to_apply: Please send a resume and cover letter to SCCCDjobs@mail.house.gov with "Summer Intern" in the subject line by March 6, 2020. Please include your approximate dates of availability in the message, and how many days per week you would be available. No calls or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer, and diverse candidates are encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of U.S. Representative Grace Meng is accepting intern applications for Summer 2020. The hours are flexible to accommodate students' hectic course schedules, but generally run 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. when Congress is in session, and 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. when not in session.

In Washington, interns' responsibilities will vary. They will be asked to answer phones, run errands, research legislation for the Member and legislative staff, attend hearings and briefings and draft constituent letters on various issues before the House. As a result, interns learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.
responsibilities: Research legislation for the Member and legislative staff
Draft constituent letters on various issues before the House
qualifications: Current student status (hours accommodate student course schedules)
Interest in learning about the legislative process and congressional office functions
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should email NY06_Intern_App@mail.house.gov a cover letter, resume, a 1-2 page writing sample, and three references.

description:
The Office of Congressman Pete Stauber is seeking full-time interns for the Summer of 2020. Responsibilities will include answering phones, managing the front office, giving Capitol tours, greeting constituents, assisting legislative staff with research, assisting with constituent letters, and other tasks as assigned.

Preferred candidates are current college students or recent graduates who have excellent customer service skills, have a strong work ethic, and pay close attention to detail. Qualified candidates will also have strong oral and written communication skills and be able to work well on a team. Minnesota ties are required, and Minnesota-08 ties are preferred.
qualifications: Current college students or recent graduates
Excellent customer service skills
Strong work ethic
Pay close attention to detail
Strong oral and written communication skills
Able to work well on a team
Minnesota ties are required
Minnesota-08 ties are preferred
how_to_apply: Interested applications should send their resume, brief cover letter, and references to Eric.Gebhart@mail.house.gov. Please indicate expected start and end dates.
salary_info: Monthly stipend; office will work with current students to receive college credits

position_title: Legislative, Communications, Counsel, and Oversight and Investigations Intern
office: House Committee on Natural Resources (Democratic Staff)
description: The Democratic Staff of the House Natural Resources Committee is seeking full-time Interns for the Summer session (May-August). Applicants must be organized, detail oriented, and dependable. Above all, we seek interns who are passionate about issues within the Natural Resources Committee's jurisdiction and demonstrate exceptional interpersonal skills. We are currently accepting applications for the following paid positions: Legislative Intern, Communications Intern, Counsel Intern, Oversight and Investigations Intern.
responsibilities: Support the Democratic Staff of the House Natural Resources Committee
Work on issues within the Natural Resources Committee's jurisdiction
Perform duties associated with legislative, communications, counsel, or oversight tracks
qualifications: Organized, detail oriented, and dependable
Passionate about issues within the Natural Resources Committee's jurisdiction
Demonstrate exceptional interpersonal skills
how_to_apply: Address application materials to Carlyn LeGrant at NRD.Internships@mail.house.gov with the subject line 'Summer Internship Application'. Include a cover letter, resume, and short writing sample. In the email, specify which internship you are applying to. The deadline for all summer applications is Friday March 6th.

office: Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs
description: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a full-time press intern for spring 2020.
responsibilities: Attending briefings and hearings for staff
Performing other tasks depending on the needs of the Committee and the intern's interests
qualifications: Strong oral and written communications skills
Desire to help veterans and learn about the House and legislative process
how_to_apply: Submit a resume and cover letter to veterans.internships@mail.house.gov. Please include in your cover letter how many days a week you would be available to intern as well as your preferred start and end dates.

description:
The Office of Rep. Abigail Spanberger (VA-07) is seeking a press intern to assist with an aggressive communications and constituent services operation for Spring 2020. This internship is a paid position.

Qualified candidates will demonstrate a strong work ethic, pay close attention to detail, and have solid writing and editing skills. Applicants must have the ability to work cooperatively in a fast-paced environment. The intern will be expected to work a minimum of 20 hours per week.
responsibilities: Assisting with the direct mail and constituent correspondence operation
Clipping videos including floor speeches and media interviews
Other duties as needed to support the communications team
qualifications: Demonstrate a strong work ethic
Pay close attention to detail
Solid writing and editing skills
Ability to work cooperatively in a fast-paced environment
Ability to work a minimum of 20 hours per week
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter and resume with dates of availability to spanbergerpressva07@gmail.com with "Spring 2020 Press Internship" in the subject line. We also recommend-but do not require-applicants submit 1-2 sample writing samples or sample graphics.
equal_opportunity: Rep. Spanberger's office is an equal opportunity employer. The office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, or uniformed service.

description:
Congressman Charlie Crist (FL-13) is seeking Full time or Part Time interns for Summer 2020 in Washington, D.C. Interns will assist the legislative and administrative staff, attending briefings, answering phones, coordinating and conducting tours, drafting constituent correspondence letters, performing legislative research and analysis, and various other tasks.

District and Florida ties are encouraged but not required.
responsibilities: Assist the legislative and administrative staff
qualifications: Availability for Summer 2020
District and Florida ties are encouraged but not required
how_to_apply: No phone calls or drop ins, please. Please email FL13.Internships@mail.house.gov, with your resume, cover letter and a 1-2 page writing sample as a single PDF.

office: Committee on House Administration Republican Office
description: The Committee on House Administration Republican Office seeks energetic and self-motivated individuals with an interest in government and public policy, to apply for its paid, full-time summer 2020 internship program. The Committee on House Administration maintains jurisdiction of federal election policy and manages the day-to-day operations of the House of Representatives with oversight of several offices within the Capitol Complex, including the Chief Administrative Officer, Architect of the Capitol, Clerk of the House, House Inspector General, Sergeant at Arms, U.S. Capitol Police, and other legislative branch entities like the Library of Congress, Government Publishing Office, and the Smithsonian Institution. Working closely with Committee staff, interns will have the opportunity to gain first-hand knowledge of the inner-workings of a congressional committee and learn about the day-to-day administrative operations of the House of Representatives.
responsibilities: Assist Committee staff with official proceedings (hearings/markups)
Provide support to all Committee staff
qualifications: Energetic and self-motivated
Interest in government and public policy
how_to_apply: To apply, please visit https://republicans-cha.house.gov/about/internshipapplication and submit your application by 2020-03-13.

office: Democratic Staff of the Committee on Science, Space and Technology
description:
The Democratic Staff of the Committee on Science, Space and Technology seeks full-time, paid interns for Summer 2020. Interns are asked to work with staff to help prepare for committee hearings and markups and will work on substantive research and oversight projects in addition to press matters.

Furthermore, interns will be critical to the daily operation of the office including tasks such as answering phones, greeting visitors, and filing official documents.
responsibilities: Help prepare for committee hearings and markups
Work on substantive research and oversight projects
qualifications: Availability for Summer 2020
Ability to help prepare for committee hearings and markups
Ability to work on substantive research and oversight projects
Ability to perform daily office operations including answering phones and greeting visitors
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should visit the Committee's website at https://science.house.gov/contact/internships. Please fill out the provided application and attach a resume. No calls or drop-ins please.

office: Democratic Staff of the Committee on Science, Space and Technology

Interns are asked to work with staff to help prepare for committee hearings and markups and will work on substantive research and oversight projects in addition to press matters. Furthermore, interns will be critical to the daily operation of the office including tasks such as answering phones, greeting visitors, and filing official documents.
responsibilities: Help prepare for committee hearings and markups

office: House Small Business Committee Minority Staff
description: The House Small Business Committee Minority Staff is currently looking for full-time interns for summer 2020 (May - August). Interns will be responsible for providing staff members with policy and administrative support. Duties may include answering phones, compiling press clips, attending staff briefings, performing legislative and policy research, and other tasks. This internship is open to undergraduates (juniors and above) and recent graduates. The office is an equal opportunity employer and minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are encouraged to apply. Please note that this internship is unpaid.
responsibilities: Providing staff members with policy and administrative support
qualifications: Current undergraduates (juniors and above)
Recent graduates
how_to_apply: Submit a one-page resume and cover letter to housesmallbusinessinternshipr@gmail.com and include "Spring 2020 Internship Application" in the subject line of the email.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are encouraged to apply.

description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee Majority Staff is currently looking for a full-time intern for the Spring 2020 semester. Interns will be responsible for providing staff members with policy and administrative support.
responsibilities: Draft memoranda and other written materials
Perform a variety of other tasks
qualifications: Team-oriented, resourceful, and upbeat
Skills beyond research and ability to work with little supervision
Undergraduates (rising seniors and above), recent graduates, and Master's and law students

In addition to providing administrative and operations support to the committee as needed, the legal intern will work closely with the Counsels team with research projects, drafting legislation, providing support for full committee markups and hearings, and attending meetings and briefings.

Provide support for full committee markups and hearings

description: Congresswoman Elaine G. Luria (VA-02) Member of the House Armed Services Committee and House Veterans Affairs Committee, where the Congresswoman serves as Chair of the Disability Assistance and Memorial Affairs Subcommittee, seeks a paid press intern for her Washington, D.C. office. The press intern will report to and work closely with the Press Assistant and the Communications Director.
responsibilities: Assist the Communications Team with other tasks as needed
qualifications: Motivated
Detail-oriented
Team player
Possess strong oral and written communication skills
Virginia ties are a plus
how_to_apply: To apply, please email a single PDF including your cover letter listing your availability, resume and a 1-2 page writing sample to VA02DC.Interns@mail.house.gov with "Your Name: VA-02 Press Internship" in the subject line. Please no drop-ins or phone calls.
equal_opportunity: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description: The Office of Representative Ro Khanna (D-CA) seeks Press/Legislative interns for the Spring 2020. The intern will be an integral part of the communications team, generating engaging content for the Member and supplying creative ideas throughout the week. This is a unique opportunity to gain both legislative and communications experience in a fast-paced and high-volume congressional office. All interns are paid $15.00 per hour for a maximum of 30 hours a week, and are expected to work collaboratively with the staff. The start date is March 23, 2020.
responsibilities: Draft social media posts for the Member's official social media pages including Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram
Create short video clips as needed
Research legislation for both the Member and legislative staff
Draft constituent letters on the Member's behalf
Guide tours of the U.S. Capitol Building
qualifications: Background in iMovie preferred
Background in the Adobe creative suite preferred
Background in TweetDeck preferred
Ability to work collaboratively with staff
how_to_apply: Applicants should send a resume, a short writing sample (no more than 2-pages), sample tweets on a current event, a sample graphic (optional) and availability to julia.albertson@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: The Office of Representative Ro Khanna is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, national origin, age, or socioeconomic status. All are welcome and encouraged to apply.

description:
Congressman Dan Newhouse (WA-04) is seeking paid interns for Summer 2020 in Washington, D.C. Interns will assist the legislative and administrative staff with various duties. Some duties include: attending briefings, answering phones, conducting Capitol tours, drafting constituent correspondence letters, and performing legislative research and analysis.

We're looking for responsible individuals that are dependable, organized, and willing to learn. Internships are open to undergraduate students and college graduates. Washington state ties are encouraged but not required.
responsibilities: Perform research using CRS, the Library of Congress, MIN, or other available resources
Perform data entry, including logging letters and constituent requests with names and addresses
Greet visitors and sign for deliveries
Note constituent opinions, including full names, addresses, and all relevant information
Attend briefings, committee meetings, intern conferences, and seminars
Perform reception duties and other office errands
Perform other duties as assigned by the Intern Supervisor
qualifications: Good oral and written communication skills
Ability to work cooperatively and courteously with others
Good organizational skills
Responsible, dependable, and willing to learn
Open to undergraduate students and college graduates
Washington state ties are encouraged but not required
how_to_apply: To apply, please email a cover letter and resume to: Johnny.Alavez@mail.house.gov

description:
The office of Congressman Jeff Van Drew (NJ-02) is currently accepting applications for full and part-time interns for the spring and summer internship sessions. The start date would be as early as Feb 15, 2020 (flexible depending on availability) with a flexible end date. Interns are paid a stipend based on availability and have access to transit benefits.

The internship will offer students and young professionals invaluable experience with our nation's legislative process. Interns contribute to the daily operations of the office and are expected to act in a reliable, professional manner at all times. Duties include (but are not limited to): greeting visitors, answering phones, leading tours of the Capitol, opening and sorting mail, attending briefings, performing legislative research, and various other tasks to assist staff. At the beginning of the legislative week, interns are expected to work into the evening to assist staff in facilitating member meetings. Congressman Van Drew supports students requesting credit for their internship through their university.
responsibilities: Assist staff with various other tasks
Work into the evening at the beginning of the legislative week to assist staff in facilitating member meetings
qualifications: Current students or young professionals
Ability to act in a reliable, professional manner at all times
Availability for flexible start and end dates
Availability to work into the evening at the beginning of the legislative week
how_to_apply: Email a resume and cover letter to JeffVanDrewNJ@gmail.com. Indicate in your cover letter that you are applying for a spring or summer internship. Format the files as [Full Name_Resume], [Full Name_Cover Letter].
salary_info: Stipend based on availability; transit benefits included
